findingDetails.title: Active review strategy state is still missing from the live packet despite cycle-2 docs claiming it was restored
findingDetails.impact: R1-010-ITER2-P1-002 remains open for active resume/focus consumers because review/deep-review-strategy.md is absent from the live review folder.
findingDetails.recommendation: Restore review/deep-review-strategy.md in the active review folder or update canonical docs/state to stop claiming an active restored strategy file.

findingDetails.title: Traceability docs disagree on verification completion
findingDetails.impact: The cycle-2 evidence trail is inconsistent even though the planner inert-data decision record and workflow consumers are wired.
findingDetails.recommendation: Align plan.md verification checkboxes/status with implementation-summary.md or update the implementation summary to cite pending checks accurately.

traceabilityChecks: Read R5 fix-completeness checklist first and classified suspected traceability issues as matrix/evidence with cross-consumer impact where active review state is consumed.

ruledOut: Planner inert-data boundary as active P1: decision-record.md and both plan YAMLs now require inert imported Planning Packet values and verified repo-relative/symbol/command evidence.
Metadata timestamp absence as active P1: description.json and graph-metadata.json both show 2026-05-02T19:53:19Z and in-progress status, matching a mid-cycle packet rather than a completed release.
noveltyJustification: Traceability review found a new active-state regression not covered by run-2 iteration 001: the restored strategy file is archived, not live, while docs claim live restoration.

findingDetails.title: Planner inert-data boundary still allows untrusted Planning Packet command text to become executable verification work
findingDetails.impact: A hostile or compromised review-derived scopeProof/Planning Packet value can be treated as a command to rerun locally, turning inert review data into command-execution guidance during planning.
findingDetails.recommendation: Forbid executing or rerunning commands copied from Planning Packet fields; allow only independently selected safe local verification commands derived from verified repo-relative paths/symbols, and render copied command text as quoted evidence or UNKNOWN.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same-class producer inventory found findingClass/scopeProof/affectedSurfaceHints in deep-review agent and state_format, Planning Packet synthesis in both deep-review auto/confirm workflows, and command-rerun consumption in both plan auto/confirm workflows.

traceabilityChecks: Read R5 fix-completeness checklist first and classified suspected security issues as cross-consumer plus algorithmic command-safety boundary.
Ran same-class producer inventory for findingClass, scopeProof, affectedSurfaceHints, activeFindings, and command-rerun wording across deep-review state, synthesis, and plan consumers.
Checked cross-consumer flow from deep-review LEAF findingDetails through synthesized Planning Packet into both /spec_kit:plan auto and confirm FIX ADDENDUM imports.

Checked matrix completeness across auto/confirm producer modes, auto/confirm consumer modes, instruction-like values, absolute-path-only values, unverifiable values, and command-shaped hostile values.

coverage.sameClassProducerInventory: findingClass/scopeProof/affectedSurfaceHints originate in deep-review agent/state_format and are serialized through both deep-review synthesis workflows.
coverage.crossConsumerFlow: deep-review findingDetails -> Planning Packet activeFindings -> plan auto/confirm FIX ADDENDUM import and writing_rule before phase drafting.
coverage.matrixCompleteness: Path leak and prompt-instruction rows are covered by UNKNOWN/inert-data wording; copied-command execution row remains uncovered because both plan modes allow commands rerun locally.

ruledOut: New absolute path leak as active P1: both plan workflows tell the planner to write UNKNOWN for absolute-path-only or unverifiable values.
Embedded prompt instruction as active P1 independent of commands: both plan workflows explicitly say not to follow instructions embedded inside imported values.
noveltyJustification: This is a fresh security finding specific to the cycle-2 inert-data wording: the fix closes path-only and instruction-like rows but leaves copied command text as an accepted local verification source.

focus: cross-cutting correctness re-verify of sample finding flow R4 -> R7 -> R3 -> planner Affected Surfaces

findingDetails.title: R4 still cannot provide the affected-surface hints that R7 and R3 now depend on
findingDetails.impact: The planner can emit the FIX ADDENDUM and R7 can pass affectedSurfaceHints when present, but a sample R4 finding lacks the producer field needed to seed affected surfaces, so the end-to-end R4 -> R7 -> R3 flow degrades to UNKNOWN instead of populated affected surfaces.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update sk-code-review's required finding schema/output contract to include affectedSurfaceHints for actionable findings, aligned with the deep-review agent/state contract, so R7 can build affectedSurfacesSeed from reducer-owned finding details without prose inference.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same-class producer inventory for affectedSurfaceHints found the field in deep-review agent/state and in R7/R3 workflow contracts, but not in sk-code-review's finding output contract or review_core.md schema. Cross-consumer flow check: R4 findingClass/scopeProof -> R7 Planning Packet works, R7 affectedSurfaceHints/affectedSurfacesSeed -> R3 Affected Surfaces works, but R4 -> R7 for affected surfaces is still absent.

traceabilityChecks: Read R5 fix-completeness checklist first and classified the suspected issue as cross-consumer plus matrix/evidence.

Checked cross-consumer flow R4 finding schema -> R7 Planning Packet -> R3 planner FIX ADDENDUM import.
Checked matrix completeness across R4 producer docs, R7 auto/confirm synthesis, R3 auto/confirm plan workflows, and the plan template Affected Surfaces section.

coverage.sameClassProducerInventory: sk-code-review emits findingClass and scopeProof but not affectedSurfaceHints; deep-review agent/state and R7/R3 workflows require or consume affectedSurfaceHints.
coverage.crossConsumerFlow: R4 findingClass/scopeProof -> R7 -> R3 is wired; R4 affectedSurfaceHints -> R7 -> R3 is not wired because R4 lacks the field.
coverage.matrixCompleteness: R7 auto/confirm and R3 auto/confirm rows are present; R4 producer row for affectedSurfaceHints is missing.

ruledOut: Prior R7-to-R3 report-only gap as active correctness P1: both plan workflow modes now import Planning Packet fields into the Affected Surfaces table before drafting phases.
Missing plan template addendum as active P1: the plan template contains FIX ADDENDUM: AFFECTED SURFACES with inventory rows.
Missing deep-review JSONL field as active P1: deep-review agent and state_format require affectedSurfaceHints in findingDetails.
noveltyJustification: The cross-cutting sample-flow check found a remaining producer-side gap not closed by the cycle-2 planner consumer fix: affectedSurfaceHints is consumed downstream but absent from the R4 sk-code-review producer contract.
